name: Elm.CodeGen

aliases.name: Declaration
aliases.comment:
The AST for a top-level Elm declaration; a function, a value, a type or a
type alias.

aliases.name: Exposing
aliases.comment: The AST for an Elm exposing statement.

aliases.name: Expression
aliases.comment: The AST for an Elm expression.

aliases.name: File
aliases.comment: The AST for an Elm file.

aliases.name: Import
aliases.comment: The AST for an Elm import statement.

aliases.name: Linkage
aliases.comment:
Linkage describes the lists of imports and exposings of a module and allows
these to be built up incrementally as code generation progresses.

aliases.name: Module
aliases.comment: The AST for an Elm module.

aliases.name: ModuleName
aliases.comment: A module name can consist of mulitple Stirngs separated with '.'.

aliases.name: Pattern
aliases.comment: The AST for a de-structuring Elm pattern matching expression.

aliases.name: TopLevelExpose
aliases.comment: The AST for a member of an Elm exposing statement.

aliases.name: TypeAnnotation
aliases.comment: The AST for an Elm type annotation.

values.name: addExposing
values.comment: Adds an exposing to the list.

values.name: addImport
values.comment: Adds an import to the list.

values.name: aliasDecl
values.type: Maybe.Maybe String.String -> String.String -> List.List String.String -> Elm.CodeGen.TypeAnnotation -> Elm.CodeGen.Declaration

values.name: chain
values.comment:
Joins multiple expressions together with the function chain operator `>>`. An
expression `a` combined with a list of expressions `[b, c, d]` results in:

    a >> b >> c >> d

values.name: combineLinkage
values.comment: Simplifies the imports and exposings by removing any duplicates.

values.name: customTypeDecl
values.type: Maybe.Maybe String.String -> String.String -> List.List String.String -> List.List ( String.String, List.List Elm.CodeGen.TypeAnnotation ) -> Elm.CodeGen.Declaration

values.name: emptyLinkage
values.comment: Creates empty imports and exposings lists.

values.name: file
values.comment:
Assembles all the components of an Elm file; the module declaration, the
comments, the imports and the top-level declarations.
values.type: Elm.CodeGen.Module -> List.List Elm.CodeGen.Import -> List.List Elm.CodeGen.Declaration -> List.List String.String -> Elm.CodeGen.File

values.name: fqVal
values.comment:
FunctionOrValue ModuleName String

Note this is the same as `fqFun`

values.name: fun
values.comment: Creates a FunctionOrValue with no qualifiying module.

values.name: funDecl
values.type: Maybe.Maybe String.String -> Maybe.Maybe Elm.Syntax.Signature.Signature -> String.String -> List.List Elm.CodeGen.Pattern -> Elm.CodeGen.Expression -> Elm.CodeGen.Declaration

values.name: importStmt
values.comment:
Creates an Elm import statement; the name of the module, an optional alias
name for the module, and an optional list of exposings from the module.
values.type: Elm.CodeGen.ModuleName -> Maybe.Maybe Elm.CodeGen.ModuleName -> Maybe.Maybe Elm.CodeGen.Exposing -> Elm.CodeGen.Import

values.name: pipe
values.comment:
Joins multiple expressions together with the pipe operator `|>`. An
expression `a` combined with a list of expressions `[b, c, d]` results in:

    a |> b |> c |> d

values.name: val
values.comment:
Creates a FunctionOrValue with no qualifiying module.

Note this is the same as `fun`.

name: Elm.Pretty
comment:
Elm.Pretty is a pretty printer for Elm syntax trees. It makes use of
`the-sett/elm-pretty-printer` to best fit the code to a given character width.

It aims to output code that is fully stable with respect to `elm-format` in the
sense that running `elm-format` on the output should have no effect at all. The
advantage of this is that if generated code moves to being edited by hand, there
will not be a large white-space only diff created when `elm-format` is applied.

To print the `Doc` created by the `pretty` functions, `the-sett/elm-pretty-printer`
is used:

    import Elm.Pretty
    import Pretty

    elmAsString =
        Elm.Pretty.pretty someFile
            |> Pretty.pretty 120

    -- Fit to a page width of 120 characters


# Pretty prints an entire Elm file.

@docs pretty


# Pretty printing snippets of Elm.

@docs prettyImports, prettyExposing
@docs prettyDeclaration, prettyFun, prettyTypeAlias, prettyCustomType, prettyPortDeclaration, prettyDestructuring
@docs prettySignature, prettyPattern, prettyExpression, prettyTypeAnnotation

values.name: pretty
values.comment: Pretty prints a file of Elm code.

values.name: prettyCustomType
values.comment: Pretty prints a custom type declaration, which may include documentation too.

values.name: prettyDeclaration
values.comment: Pretty prints a single top-level declaration.

values.name: prettyExposing
values.comment:
Pretty prints the contents of an exposing statement, as found on a module or import
statement.

The exposed values will be de-duplicated and sorted.

values.name: prettyFun
values.comment: Pretty prints an Elm function, which may include documentation and a signature too.

values.name: prettyImports
values.comment:
Pretty prints a list of import statements.

The list will be de-duplicated and sorted.

values.name: prettyTypeAlias
values.comment: Pretty prints a type alias definition, which may include documentation too.
